summary: Build and exercise one native PyInstaller-onedir sidecar bootstrap through the existing signed installed-slot launch boundary.
rationale: Issue #372 adds only a production-unreachable packaged bootstrap. The build tool emits an internal test-only release manifest and detached Ed25519 signature, while existing public admission derives product, main, sidecar, executable-hash, and protocol facts from that verified manifest. The resolver now checks every declared onedir sidecar file before launch; Windows retains its prior opened-executable and suspended-child binding. No browser, WTSCLI, journal, operation DTO, production routing, handshake, or platform-production-signing path is introduced.
dependency_rationale: PyInstaller 6.21.0 is pinned only in the development build toolchain so each native CI runner can build the self-contained onedir bootstrap; it is not a runtime dependency of the packaged child.
deletion_targets: fixture or system-Python sidecar launch evidence presented as a packaged artifact
caller-supplied product, main, sidecar, artifact-hash, or protocol identity at the launch boundary
unsigned, wheel, dual-runtime, browser, WTSCLI, journal, and handshake paths in the packaged bootstrap slice
verification: uv run ruff check scripts/build_offline_macos_intel.py tests/test_build_offline_macos_intel.py tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run ty check scripts/build_offline_macos_intel.py tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_build_offline_macos_intel.py tests/test_pr_governance.py

uv run --group dev python -m pytest -p no:tach tests/test_installed_release.py tests/test_installed_release_admission.py tests/test_installed_slot_lease.py tests/test_owned_sidecar_process.py tests/test_windows_installed_binding.py tests/test_windows_sidecar_process.py tests/test_packaged_sidecar_artifact.py -q
uv run --group dev ruff check src tests tools
uv run --group dev ty check src tests tools
uv run --group dev python tools/check_privacy_gate.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_agent_safety_gate.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_ai_bad_smells.py --base origin/main
risks: The CI signer is an intentionally public test-only Ed25519 key; it is neither Apple Developer ID/notarization nor Windows Authenticode and cannot be promoted to a production trust policy.

macOS runtime-image authenticity and installer-owned immutable slots remain later production gates; this slice verifies the entire declared onedir tree immediately before launch and preserves the existing Windows executable-object binding.
The bootstrap has no authenticated peer or workflow authority. The four-step handshake and all production callers remain separate work.
